kicker: RAD SAFETY // MATERIALS LICENSE · OSHA · THE BOUNDARY

beats.narration: Two regulators stand over every radiography job. One owns the source. The other owns the scaffold, the vessel, and the chemicals around it.

payload.title: TWO REGULATORS ON EVERY SHOT
payload.sub: The materials license. OSHA. One job site, divided by hazard.

beats.narration: Radiography never runs on a general license. You file NRC Form 313, reviewed against Part 30.33, and a specific license comes back.

payload.heading: Only a specific license will do

beats.narration: The 34.13 package spells out your whole safety program. Procedures, the RSO with two thousand hours, six-month inspections, every location of use.
And the line between agencies. The license owns the licensed source. OSHA owns conventional hazards and machine radiation. Paragraph p deems a compliant licensee compliant.

payload.heading: Two agencies, one job site
payload.note: LICENSE = LICENSED SOURCE · OSHA = FALLS, CHEMICALS, X-RAY MACHINES · 1910.1096(p) DEEMS YOU COMPLIANT

beats.narration: The application becomes the license, so amend before you drift. OSHA owns everything else. Know which inspector owns which hazard.

payload.sub: Field practice that strays from the submitted procedure is a license violation, even with no dose exceeded.
